Best India to Japan Tour Package – Explore the Land of the Rising Sun

Embark on an enchanting voyage through Japan, where ancient temples stand beside neon-lit skyscrapers and serene bamboo forests give way to cutting-edge technology. With Travelxploria as your Best Travel Agency and trusted Best International Tour Operator, experience a seamless blend of heritage, adventure, and romance—crafted for honeymooners, families, and couples alike. Our best budget India to Japan tour package offers competitive Japan tour package prices starting from ₹1,25,000, providing excellent value for an unforgettable experience. Explore all options in our extensive Tour Package India collection.

Why Choose a Japan Tour Package from India?

 Unique Blend of Tradition and Technology
Begin your journey in Tokyo, the pulsating heart of modern Japan. Ascend the Tokyo Skytree for panoramic cityscapes, then wander the bustling streets of Shibuya and Akihabara. As the Best Tour Operator, Travelxploria offers skip-the-line access and private guides, ensuring your Best value tour package captures both the towering architecture and hidden alley-way sushi gems. For a Couple Package, enjoy a twilight cruise on the Sumida River, watching lanterns drift beneath illuminated bridges. Experience the perfect fusion of ancient tradition and cutting-edge technology unique to Japan. For other romantic options, see our popular honeymoon packages.

Visa Support and Direct Flights from India
Travel with peace of mind knowing that our packages include comprehensive visa assistance and easy access to direct flights from major Indian cities, such as Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Kerala, and Kolkata. This ensures hassle-free travel and a smooth start to your adventure.

Ideal for Families, Solo Travelers, and Culture Lovers
Whether traveling solo, with family, or as a couple, Japan’s diverse offerings appeal to all. Our Japan tour packages from India cater to varied interests, from cultural immersion and sightseeing to culinary experiences and nature exploration. Discover tailored options through our family tour packages and solo travel packages. Adventure seekers will enjoy exclusive adventure tour packages as well.

Top India to Japan Tour Packages

7 to 10 Days Japan Tour Package from India – Tokyo, Kyoto & Osaka
Our popular Japan package covers Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, including an exciting India to Japan tour package with bullet train ride experience. Travel aboard the famous Shinkansen and enjoy speedy, scenic connections between cities. Whether booking a Japan tour package from Delhi, Mumbai, or Chennai, you’re guaranteed an expertly curated itinerary packed with highlights.

Japan Honeymoon Packages for Indian Couples
Celebrate your love with our India to Japan honeymoon tour package, featuring romantic ryokans, private onsen visits, and exclusive experiences. Enjoy scenic views and intimate moments, all at an affordable India to Japan tour package price.

Budget-Friendly Japan Tour Packages from India
Looking for an affordable India to Japan tour package? Travelxploria’s best budget India to Japan tour package combines quality and savings without compromise, making Japan accessible to more travelers.

Customized Japan Itineraries for Families and Groups
Tailored for families and groups, our packages allow you to choose activities and experiences that suit your needs, with expert guidance on kid-friendly spots and group discounts.

Must-Visit Places Included in Japan Tour Packages

Tokyo – Skytree, Shibuya Crossing & Anime Culture
Explore Tokyo’s iconic landmarks and vibrant neighborhoods with ease, benefiting from local guides who share insider tips.

Kyoto – Shrines, Geisha District & Bamboo Forest
Discover timeless shrines, traditional geisha districts, and the serene Arashiyama Bamboo Grove. Early visits help avoid crowds, ensuring a peaceful experience.

Osaka – Universal Studios, Street Food & Shopping
Dive into Osaka’s culinary delights like takoyaki and okonomiyaki, with exclusive cooking classes included in select family packages.

Mount Fuji – Day Trip & Sightseeing
Capture unforgettable views of Mount Fuji with our special photo tours and bento breakfasts.

Nara & Hiroshima – Deer Park & Peace Memorial
Reflect on history at Hiroshima’s Peace Memorial and enjoy Nara’s friendly deer, all arranged by your trusted Best Tour Operator.

What’s Included in the Best India to Japan Tour Packages?

1. Round-Trip Flights from Major Indian Cities
2. Accommodation with Daily Breakfast
3. Japan Rail Pass, Local Transfers & Guided Tours
4. Visa Assistance and Travel Insurance


Best Time to Visit Japan from India

March to May – Cherry Blossom (Sakura) Season
Experience the breathtaking sakura season, the most popular time to visit Japan.

October to November – Autumn Colors & Pleasant Weather
Enjoy comfortable temperatures and vibrant fall foliage during this ideal travel window.

Tips for Avoiding Crowds and Getting the Best Deals
Travel in shoulder seasons or weekdays for better rates and a more relaxed experience.

Frequently Asked Questions About India to Japan Tour Packages

Q1: Is a visa required for Indian citizens to visit Japan?
Yes, but with Travelxploria’s comprehensive visa support, the process is smooth and hassle-free.

Q2: How many days are ideal for a Japan tour?
A 7 to 10 days itinerary offers the perfect balance of sightseeing and leisure.

Q3: Is Japan expensive for Indian travelers?
While Japan can be pricey, our affordable India to Japan tour package options and budget tips make it accessible.

Q4: What is the India to Japan honeymoon tour package?
Our honeymoon packages include romantic experiences such as private onsens and scenic cruises, tailored to couples seeking intimacy and luxury.

Q5: What does an India to Japan tour package with bullet train ride include?
Travel aboard the Shinkansen, enjoying fast and comfortable journeys between Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, with scenic views along the way.

How to Book the Best India to Japan Tour Package

Top-Rated Indian Travel Agencies Offering Japan Packages
Choose Travelxploria for expert service, transparent pricing, and flexible bookings.

Online Booking Platforms and Exclusive Deals
Leverage online platforms for special discounts and last-minute deals. Consider planning your next trip with our popular India to Dubai Tour package or tropical India to Bali Tour Package.

Important Travel Documents and Cultural Tips
Prepare your passport, visa, insurance, and learn key Japanese etiquette such as bowing and shoe removal customs for a smooth trip.

packages.overview:
• Experience breathtaking views of Mount Fuji, a scenic lake cruise, and a traditional stay at a Japanese Ryokan.
  • Explore a restored traditional village, marvel at Shiraito Falls, visit the Toyota Museum, and arrive in historic Kyoto.
  • Immerse yourself in Kyoto’s rich heritage with visits to temples, bamboo groves, shrines, and the iconic Gion district.
  • Wander the charming post-town of Tsumago, visit Matsumoto’s striking black castle, and return to Tokyo by evening.

Today, we’ll immerse ourselves in the rich cultural heritage of Kyoto, Japan’s imperial capital from 794 to 1868. Remarkably untouched during World War II, Kyoto has preserved its artistic treasures. Our journey begins in Arashiyama, where we’ll admire the scenic Togetsukyo Bridge and visit the revered Tenryuji Temple, followed by a peaceful walk through the enchanting bamboo forest. We’ll then explore Fushimi Inari Shrine, the Imperial Palace, and the stunning Golden Pavilion (Kinkakuji Temple). In the evening, we’ll transfer you to Gion, Kyoto’s traditional district. Enjoy free time here, with the return to the hotel on your own by taxi.

At 07:30 a.m., we’ll depart from Kyoto and begin our scenic journey through Japan’s picturesque countryside toward the historic village of Tsumago. Arriving around 11:15 a.m., you’ll discover this charming wooden town, widely regarded as one of Japan’s most beautiful. Enjoy some free time to explore its preserved Edo-period streets before savoring a traditional lunch at a local restaurant. At 01:30 p.m., we’ll continue to Matsumoto, where we’ll arrive at 02:45 p.m. to visit the iconic Matsumoto Castle, known as “The Crow” for its striking black façade. After some leisure time, we’ll depart at 05:00 p.m. and reach Tokyo by 08:30–09:00 p.m.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrive in Tokyo, meet our Travelxploria Representative, and transfer to the hotel for a relaxed day at leisure.
  • Explore Tokyo's top landmarks including Zojoji Temple, Shibuya Crossing, Meiji Shrine, and Asakusa, with lunch and dinner included.
  • Travel to Mount Fuji and Kawaguchiko with stunning views, a lake cruise, and traditional Ryokan stay with onsen.
  • Discover a traditional mountain village, Shiraito Falls, and the Toyota Museum before arriving in Kyoto.
  • Full-day sightseeing in Kyoto, visiting temples, shrines, bamboo forests, and the famous Gion district.
  • Visit Todaiji Temple, Horyuji Temple, and transfer to Osaka with an evening in lively Dotonbori.
  • Enjoy breakfast and bid farewell to Japan with cherished memories, assisted by your Travelxploria Representative.

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

Today, we’ll immerse ourselves in the rich cultural heritage of Kyoto, Japan’s imperial capital from 794 to 1868. Remarkably untouched during World War II, Kyoto has preserved its artistic treasures. Our journey begins in Arashiyama, where we’ll admire the scenic Togetsukyo Bridge and visit the revered Tenryuji Temple, followed by a peaceful walk through the enchanting bamboo forest. We’ll then explore Fushimi Inari Shrine, the Imperial Palace, and the stunning Golden Pavilion (Kinkakuji Temple). In the evening, we’ll transfer you to Gion, Kyoto’s traditional district. Enjoy free time here, with the return to the hotel on your own by taxi.

At 8:00 a.m., we’ll depart from Kyoto and travel to Nara, arriving by 9:00 a.m. to visit the iconic Todaiji Temple, built in 752 and home to a majestic giant Buddha statue. Surrounded by a peaceful park with friendly, free-roaming deer, it’s a perfect spot for photos and reflection. At 10:30 a.m., we’ll head to Horyuji Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site with ancient wooden structures and deep spiritual heritage. After exploring, we’ll enjoy lunch before continuing to Osaka at 2:30 p.m. In the evening, we’ll visit Dotonbori, where you can experience its vibrant nightlife, neon lights, and delicious cuisine.

packages.overview:
• Arrival in Tokyo with a shared transfer to the hotel and free time to relax.
  • Explore Tokyo’s iconic landmarks on a guided city tour with lunch and dinner included.
  • Discover Kawaguchiko and ascend Mount Fuji, with a stay at a traditional Ryokan.
  • Visit a heritage village, scenic falls, Toyota Museum, and arrive in Kyoto.
  • Full-day tour of Kyoto's temples, shrines, bamboo forest, and vibrant Gion district.
  • Visit Nara’s giant Buddha, Horyuji Temple, and end the day exploring Osaka.
  • Tour Himeji Castle and Korakuen Garden with free time in Okayama.
  • Stroll through Kurashiki, visit Hiroshima’s memorials, and explore Miyajima Island.
  • Departure day with cherished memories of your Japanese journey.
4o

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Tokyo, meet the Travelxploria Representative, and transfer to your hotel with the rest of the day at leisure.
  • Explore Tokyo’s iconic landmarks on a guided city tour with visits to temples, shrines, gardens, and vibrant districts, followed by a night out in Shinjuku.
  • Travel to Kawaguchiko and Mount Fuji, enjoy stunning views, a boat ride, and a traditional Ryokan experience with onsen and dinner.
  • Visit Iyashi no Sato Nenba, the breathtaking Shiraito Falls, the Toyota Museum, and arrive in Kyoto in the evening.
  • Discover Kyoto’s rich heritage with visits to temples, bamboo forests, shrines, and the geisha district of Gion.
  • Journey through rural Japan visiting the traditional village of Tsumago and historic Matsumoto Castle before returning to Tokyo.
  • Conclude your unforgettable journey with cherished memories as you depart from Japan.

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

packages.overview:
• Arrival in Tokyo with transfer by shared shuttle; free time and tour briefing by Travelxploria Representative.
  • Guided tour of Tokyo’s highlights including temples, Shibuya Crossing, and Asakusa, with lunch and Shinjuku dinner included.
  • Visit iconic Mount Fuji, enjoy a boat ride on Lake Kawaguchiko, and stay overnight in a traditional Ryokan with dinner.
  • Explore a traditional village, Shiraito Falls, and the Toyota Museum en route to Kyoto.
  • Full-day Kyoto sightseeing including bamboo forest, Golden Pavilion, Fushimi Inari, and traditional Gion district.
  • After breakfast, Travelxploria Representative assists with departure, concluding your unforgettable journey.

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Tokyo and enjoy a leisure day with airport transfer and tour briefing.
  • Explore Tokyo's iconic sites including Shibuya Crossing, Meiji Shrine, and Asakusa, with lunch and dinner included.
  • Visit Mount Fuji and Kawaguchiko, enjoy scenic views, a lake cruise, and a stay in a traditional Ryokan.
  • Explore a traditional village, admire Shiraito Falls, visit the Toyota Museum, and arrive in Kyoto.
  • Discover Kyoto’s famous landmarks including Arashiyama, Fushimi Inari Shrine, and Gion.
  • Travel through rural Japan visiting the historic village of Tsumago and Matsumoto Castle.
  • Visit Zenkoji Temple, see snow monkeys, stroll Kusatsu spa town, and stay in a Ryokan.
  • Explore Nikko’s shrines and scenic abyss before returning to Tokyo.
  • Departure after breakfast with memories of a remarkable Japanese journey.

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

Today, we’ll immerse ourselves in the rich cultural heritage of Kyoto, Japan’s imperial capital from 794 to 1868. Remarkably untouched during World War II, Kyoto has preserved its artistic treasures. Our journey begins in Arashiyama, where we’ll admire the scenic Togetsukyo Bridge and visit the revered Tenryuji Temple, followed by a peaceful walk through the enchanting bamboo forest. We’ll then explore Fushimi Inari Shrine, the Imperial Palace, and the stunning Golden Pavilion (Kinkakuji Temple). In the evening, we’ll transfer you to Gion, Kyoto’s traditional district. Enjoy free time here, with the return to the hotel on your own by taxi.

packages.overview:
• Arrival in Tokyo; transfer to hotel and leisure time to explore on your own.
  • Guided city tour covering iconic sights like Shibuya Crossing, Meiji Shrine, and Sensoji Temple, with dinner in Shinjuku.
  • Ride the bullet train to Hiroshima, visit the Peace Memorial Park, and ferry to Miyajima Island to explore Itsukushima Shrine.
  • Scenic ferry ride to Matsuyama with visits to Matsuyama Castle, Ishiteji Temple, and traditional Dogo Onsen.
  • Shrine visit, garden stroll, and walk above sea whirlpools before arriving in Kobe.
  • Visit the Earthquake Memorial Museum and stay overnight in a Buddhist monastery with Zen meditation.
  • Full-day Kyoto city tour including Golden Pavilion, Fushimi Inari Shrine, and the Geisha district of Gion.
  • Departure with unforgettable memories of Japan.

This morning, embark on an extensive guided tour of Tokyo, lasting approximately five hours, exploring the city’s vibrant energy and peaceful charm. Begin with a brief stop at Zojoji Temple, offering a stunning view of Tokyo Tower and featuring hundreds of Jizo statues, protectors of children. Continue to the iconic Shibuya Crossing, followed by a visit to the Meiji Shrine, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. Travel along the stylish Omotesando Avenue, admire the Imperial Palace gardens and Nijubashi Bridge, and pass through Akihabara, Tokyo’s hub for anime and electronics, and Kokugikan Sumo Arena. Conclude the tour at the historic Sensoji Temple and Nakamise Street. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant before returning to the hotel. In the evening, meet your guide at 6:30 p.m. and travel by metro to explore Shinjuku’s dynamic nightlife, followed by dinner at a local restaurant. Return to the hotel by metro, accompanied by your guide.

Today, we cover a total distance of 860 km, including 800 km by high-speed bullet train. At 07:30 a.m., depart from the hotel and transfer to Tokyo Station to board the bullet train for a scenic four-hour journey to Hiroshima. If the group has fewer than 15 people, you will travel without a guide, who will meet you at Hiroshima Station. Upon arrival around 12:30 p.m., visit Hiroshima’s poignant Peace Memorial Park, the Atomic Bomb Dome, and the powerful Peace Memorial Museum. After some free time for lunch, we’ll head to the outskirts of Hiroshima and take a 15-minute ferry ride to Miyajima Island—famed for its spiritual beauty and the iconic Itsukushima Shrine, which stands elegantly over the sea. Enjoy time to explore the shrine and stroll through the charming island center. At 6:00 p.m., we’ll return by ferry and continue to our hotel, where dinner will be served.

Today, we embark on a scenic journey by ferry, covering approximately 50 km as we travel to Shikoku Island. Departing from the hotel at 08:00 a.m., we’ll board the ferry at 09:20 a.m. for a relaxing two-and-a-half-hour cruise through a beautiful sea passage dotted with islands—perfect for enjoying from the deck, so bring warm clothing. Upon arriving in Matsuyama at 12:00 p.m., we’ll ascend by cable car or chairlift to visit Matsuyama Castle, a historic hilltop fortress offering panoramic views. We’ll then explore Ishiteji Temple, a sacred Buddhist pilgrimage site rich in history and architectural charm. A delicious lunch will be included. In the afternoon, we’ll visit the famous Dogo Onsen, one of Japan’s oldest traditional bathhouses. We encourage you to embrace the unique Onsen culture—bathing customs will be explained. Please note that some Onsen may restrict entry to guests with tattoos, and bathing is traditionally done without clothing.

Today, we travel 345 km through scenic mountainous landscapes and across stunning suspension bridges. We depart Matsuyama at 07:30 a.m. and arrive in Kotohira by 10:00 a.m. Here, we’ll visit a revered Shinto shrine dedicated to the God of sailors, located atop Mount Zozu. The shrine can be reached by climbing 785 steps, but for those who prefer to stay at the base, there’s a vibrant tourist area and a small sake museum to enjoy. At 12:00 p.m., we depart for Takamatsu and visit the exquisite Ritsurin Garden, one of Japan’s most beautiful traditional gardens. Lunch will be included at a local restaurant. At 03:00 p.m., we head to Naruto, where we’ll explore a spectacular bridge with a glass floor, offering views of the famous sea whirlpools from 45 meters above (tide permitting). We then continue through dramatic sea-crossing bridges and arrive in Kobe by 07:00 p.m. for an evening stroll in its vibrant downtown.

Today’s journey covers 140 km and begins after breakfast with a visit to the Earthquake Memorial Museum in Kobe, honoring the victims of the devastating 1995 earthquake and showcasing the city's inspiring recovery. At 10:30 a.m., we depart Kobe and head toward Osaka, Japan’s second-largest city, making a brief technical stop if required. By 11:30 a.m., we depart for Mount Koya, the spiritual heart of Shingon Buddhism in Japan. Along the way, we stop for lunch (included). Upon arrival, we explore the Okuno Mausoleum—a sacred cemetery nestled in the forest with over 200,000 tombstones—and visit the historic Kongobuji Temple, founded in 1593. We’ll spend the night in a traditional Buddhist monastery, where monks will guide us through a Zen meditation session (Ajikan), followed by a vegetarian dinner. Note: Rooms are traditional with shared bathrooms, and meditation may not be guaranteed during peak dates. Alternate accommodation in nearby hotels may be arranged if necessary.

Arriving by 10:30 a.m., we’ll spend the day exploring this culturally rich city, which served as Japan’s capital from 794 to 1868. Spared from bombing during World War II, Kyoto retains a vast wealth of historic architecture and cultural treasures. Our guided tour includes visits to the stunning Kinkakuji Temple, known as the Golden Pavilion, surrounded by beautifully landscaped gardens, and the Imperial Palace. After free time for lunch, we’ll visit the iconic Fushimi Inari Shrine, famous for its thousands of vibrant red torii gates. We then transfer to Gion, Kyoto’s historic Geisha district, where you’ll have time to explore at your own pace. Return to the hotel is unaccompanied—your guide will provide detailed instructions. Note: If you’ve previously toured Kyoto, the guide can recommend alternate experiences.​

packages.overview:
• ​Tokyo: Arrive in Tokyo and check in at your hotel for a relaxing evening after your journey.
  • ​Tokyo – Oishi Park – Lake Kawaguchi – Mount Fuji – Hamamatsu: Discover Lake Kawaguchi’s serene beauty, view majestic Mount Fuji, and explore Oshino Hakkai village.
  • Hamamatsu – Nagoya – Dōtonbori – Osaka: Visit the historic Nagoya Castle and enjoy vibrant Dōtonbori’s lights, street food, and shopping.
  • Osaka – Kyoto – Gion – Osaka: Explore Kyoto's Fushimi Inari Shrine, Golden Pavilion, Bamboo Grove, and the historic Gion district.
  • ​Osaka – Todaiji – Igaryu – Shizuoka: Visit Nara’s Todaiji Temple and explore the fascinating Ninja Museum of Igaryu en route to Shizuoka.
  • ​Shizuoka – Asakusa – Nakamise Street – Tokyo: Tour Tokyo’s Asakusa neighborhood, Sensō-ji Temple, Nakamise Street, and enjoy panoramic city views.
  • Tokyo: Enjoy a final breakfast before departing Japan or continuing your journey independently.​

After breakfast, travel to Nagoya, home to the historic Nagoya Castle, a stunning 17th-century structure and the city’s most iconic landmark. Take time to explore the castle’s impressive architecture and rich history. From there, continue to Osaka, where you’ll embark on a walking tour of the lively Dōtonbori area. This bustling district, located along the Dōtonbori Canal, is renowned for its vibrant street food scene, shopping, bars, and entertainment. packages.overview:
• Arrive in Tokyo and check in at your hotel; evening at leisure to unwind and prepare for your adventure.
  • Begin your tour with stunning views of Mount Fuji, Oishi Park, and Oshino Hakkai before heading to Hamamatsu.
  • Visit the iconic Nagoya Castle, then explore the lively Dōtonbori district in Osaka.
  • Discover Kyoto’s cultural treasures including Fushimi Inari Shrine, Golden Pavilion, Bamboo Grove, and Gion district.
  • Explore Nara’s Todaiji Temple and the Igaryu Ninja Museum en route to Shizuoka.
  • Return to Tokyo to explore historic Asakusa, Sensō-ji Temple, and enjoy panoramic city views.
  • Enjoy your final breakfast and depart for the airport or continue your onward journey.

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Sapporo, make your own way to the hotel, and relax for the evening.
  • Enjoy a full-day guided tour to the charming port city of Otaru with highlights including the Otaru Canal and Tanaka Sake Brewery.
  • Board a scenic train journey to Hakodate and spend your afternoon at leisure.
  • Explore Hakodate’s vibrant morning market, Red Brick Warehouses, Motomachi District, and historic Goryokaku Fort on a guided tour.
  • Travel via bullet train to Sendai and enjoy a relaxing afternoon at leisure.
  • Take a half-day tour to Matsushima to visit the ancient Zuiganji Temple and enjoy a cruise through the scenic bay.
  • Board the train to Tokyo and enjoy the rest of the day exploring Japan’s bustling capital at your own pace.
  • Your memorable northern Japan journey ends; transfer independently to the airport or continue exploring.

overview:
Best India to Japan Tour Package – Explore the Land of the Rising Sun

Embark on an enchanting voyage through Japan, where ancient temples stand beside neon-lit skyscrapers and serene bamboo forests give way to cutting-edge technology. With Travelxploria as your Best Travel Agency and trusted Best International Tour Operator, experience a seamless blend of heritage, adventure, and romance—crafted for honeymooners, families, and couples alike. Our best budget India to Japan tour package offers competitive Japan tour package prices starting from ₹1,25,000, providing excellent value for an unforgettable experience. Explore all options in our extensive Tour Package India collection.

Why Choose a Japan Tour Package from India?

 Unique Blend of Tradition and Technology
Begin your journey in Tokyo, the pulsating heart of modern Japan. Ascend the Tokyo Skytree for panoramic cityscapes, then wander the bustling streets of Shibuya and Akihabara. As the Best Tour Operator, Travelxploria offers skip-the-line access and private guides, ensuring your Best value tour package captures both the towering architecture and hidden alley-way sushi gems. For a Couple Package, enjoy a twilight cruise on the Sumida River, watching lanterns drift beneath illuminated bridges. Experience the perfect fusion of ancient tradition and cutting-edge technology unique to Japan. For other romantic options, see our popular honeymoon packages.

Visa Support and Direct Flights from India
Travel with peace of mind knowing that our packages include comprehensive visa assistance and easy access to direct flights from major Indian cities, such as Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Kerala, and Kolkata. This ensures hassle-free travel and a smooth start to your adventure.

Ideal for Families, Solo Travelers, and Culture Lovers
Whether traveling solo, with family, or as a couple, Japan’s diverse offerings appeal to all. Our Japan tour packages from India cater to varied interests, from cultural immersion and sightseeing to culinary experiences and nature exploration. Discover tailored options through our family tour packages and solo travel packages. Adventure seekers will enjoy exclusive adventure tour packages as well.

Top India to Japan Tour Packages

7 to 10 Days Japan Tour Package from India – Tokyo, Kyoto & Osaka
Our popular Japan package covers Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, including an exciting India to Japan tour package with bullet train ride experience. Travel aboard the famous Shinkansen and enjoy speedy, scenic connections between cities. Whether booking a Japan tour package from Delhi, Mumbai, or Chennai, you’re guaranteed an expertly curated itinerary packed with highlights.

Japan Honeymoon Packages for Indian Couples
Celebrate your love with our India to Japan honeymoon tour package, featuring romantic ryokans, private onsen visits, and exclusive experiences. Enjoy scenic views and intimate moments, all at an affordable India to Japan tour package price.

Budget-Friendly Japan Tour Packages from India
Looking for an affordable India to Japan tour package? Travelxploria’s best budget India to Japan tour package combines quality and savings without compromise, making Japan accessible to more travelers.

Customized Japan Itineraries for Families and Groups
Tailored for families and groups, our packages allow you to choose activities and experiences that suit your needs, with expert guidance on kid-friendly spots and group discounts.

Must-Visit Places Included in Japan Tour Packages

Tokyo – Skytree, Shibuya Crossing & Anime Culture
Explore Tokyo’s iconic landmarks and vibrant neighborhoods with ease, benefiting from local guides who share insider tips.

Kyoto – Shrines, Geisha District & Bamboo Forest
Discover timeless shrines, traditional geisha districts, and the serene Arashiyama Bamboo Grove. Early visits help avoid crowds, ensuring a peaceful experience.

Osaka – Universal Studios, Street Food & Shopping
Dive into Osaka’s culinary delights like takoyaki and okonomiyaki, with exclusive cooking classes included in select family packages.

Mount Fuji – Day Trip & Sightseeing
Capture unforgettable views of Mount Fuji with our special photo tours and bento breakfasts.

Nara & Hiroshima – Deer Park & Peace Memorial
Reflect on history at Hiroshima’s Peace Memorial and enjoy Nara’s friendly deer, all arranged by your trusted Best Tour Operator.

What’s Included in the Best India to Japan Tour Packages?

1. Round-Trip Flights from Major Indian Cities
2. Accommodation with Daily Breakfast
3. Japan Rail Pass, Local Transfers & Guided Tours
4. Visa Assistance and Travel Insurance


Best Time to Visit Japan from India

March to May – Cherry Blossom (Sakura) Season
Experience the breathtaking sakura season, the most popular time to visit Japan.

October to November – Autumn Colors & Pleasant Weather
Enjoy comfortable temperatures and vibrant fall foliage during this ideal travel window.

Tips for Avoiding Crowds and Getting the Best Deals
Travel in shoulder seasons or weekdays for better rates and a more relaxed experience.

Frequently Asked Questions About India to Japan Tour Packages

Q1: Is a visa required for Indian citizens to visit Japan?
Yes, but with Travelxploria’s comprehensive visa support, the process is smooth and hassle-free.

Q2: How many days are ideal for a Japan tour?
A 7 to 10 days itinerary offers the perfect balance of sightseeing and leisure.

Q3: Is Japan expensive for Indian travelers?
While Japan can be pricey, our affordable India to Japan tour package options and budget tips make it accessible.

Q4: What is the India to Japan honeymoon tour package?
Our honeymoon packages include romantic experiences such as private onsens and scenic cruises, tailored to couples seeking intimacy and luxury.

Q5: What does an India to Japan tour package with bullet train ride include?
Travel aboard the Shinkansen, enjoying fast and comfortable journeys between Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka, with scenic views along the way.

How to Book the Best India to Japan Tour Package

Top-Rated Indian Travel Agencies Offering Japan Packages
Choose Travelxploria for expert service, transparent pricing, and flexible bookings.

Online Booking Platforms and Exclusive Deals
Leverage online platforms for special discounts and last-minute deals. Consider planning your next trip with our popular India to Dubai Tour package or tropical India to Bali Tour Package.

Important Travel Documents and Cultural Tips
Prepare your passport, visa, insurance, and learn key Japanese etiquette such as bowing and shoe removal customs for a smooth trip.
